note: Registry entries summarize implementable driven neural-field examples without copying paper text or figures. Private PDFs, page renders, and extraction notes remain under private/papers.

examples.label: MacKay rays from localized half-space input
examples.paper: Tamekue, Prandi, and Chitour driven neural-field MacKay examples
examples.mathematical_object: stationary scalar neural field a = I + mu * omega * f(a)
examples.domain: finite square visual-field diagnostic grid on [-10, 10]^2
examples.symmetry_assumption: localized half-space perturbation of periodic grating input
examples.input_type: cosine grating plus small localized step input
examples.method: linear fixed-point iteration with finite zero-padded separable Gaussian convolution
examples.expected_behavior: generated first-pass ray-like modulation near the localized forcing boundary
examples.rights_status: generated outputs only; source figures remain private
examples.missing_evidence: source-target numeric figure extraction and calibrated boundary model

examples.label: MacKay target from localized strip input
examples.paper: Tamekue, Prandi, and Chitour driven neural-field MacKay examples
examples.mathematical_object: stationary scalar neural field a = I + mu * omega * f(a)
examples.domain: finite square visual-field diagnostic grid on [-10, 10]^2
examples.symmetry_assumption: localized strip perturbation of periodic grating input
examples.input_type: cosine grating plus small horizontal strip and boundary steps
examples.method: linear fixed-point iteration with finite zero-padded separable Gaussian convolution
examples.expected_behavior: generated first-pass target-like modulation around the localized strip
examples.rights_status: generated outputs only; source figures remain private
examples.missing_evidence: source-target numeric figure extraction and calibrated boundary model

examples.label: MacKay localized input Gaussian-only negative check
examples.paper: Tamekue, Prandi, and Chitour driven neural-field MacKay examples
examples.figure_or_example: control run for the MacKay localized-input examples
examples.mathematical_object: stationary scalar neural field with excitatory-only Gaussian coupling
examples.domain: finite square visual-field diagnostic grid on [-10, 10]^2
examples.symmetry_assumption: same rays input as the difference-of-Gaussians diagnostic
examples.input_type: cosine grating plus small localized step input
examples.method: linear fixed-point iteration with finite zero-padded separable Gaussian convolution
examples.expected_behavior: control output should be smoother and less edge-selective than the DoG run
examples.rights_status: generated outputs only; source figures remain private
examples.missing_evidence: formal acceptance threshold against a private source-derived target

examples.label: Nicks 1D spatial-forcing resonance tongues
examples.paper: Nicks et al. sensory-induced hallucination spatial-forcing analysis
examples.figure_or_example: one-dimensional forced response and tongue diagnostics
examples.domain: one-dimensional periodic or large finite cortical coordinate
examples.symmetry_assumption: weak spatial forcing near selected cortical modes
examples.kernel_family: translation-invariant neural-field kernel with Fourier-mode gains
examples.method: linear stability and continuation of forced equilibria
examples.expected_behavior: resonance tongue map and response-mode labels
examples.rights_status: source figures remain private pending explicit license path
examples.missing_evidence: public-safe numeric target table and finalized continuation grid

examples.label: Nicks 2D orthogonal response amplitude equations
examples.paper: Nicks et al. sensory-induced hallucination spatial-forcing analysis
examples.mathematical_object: coupled amplitude equations for forced pattern modes
examples.domain: two-dimensional cortical domain with Fourier-mode truncation
examples.kernel_family: mode-gain abstraction from a neural-field kernel
examples.parameter_summary: amplitude-equation coefficients require private extraction normalization
examples.expected_behavior: orthogonal-response region labels and representative generated fields
examples.rights_status: source figures remain private pending explicit license path
examples.missing_evidence: coefficient normalization and source-target acceptance criteria

examples.label: Nicks Billock-Tsou-style generated response map
examples.paper: Nicks et al. sensory-induced hallucination spatial-forcing analysis
examples.mathematical_object: forced neural field or reduced amplitude-equation response map
examples.symmetry_assumption: localized or grating-driven response modes after Rule
examples.kernel_family: paper-specific neural-field kernel or reduced mode-gain surrogate
examples.parameter_summary: requires extracted source-target axes before implementation
examples.method: source-target comparison using generated numeric fields
examples.expected_behavior: generated map for comparison against Billock-Tsou-style qualitative targets
examples.rights_status: source figures remain private pending explicit license path

examples.label: Bolelli localized periodic-input attractor
examples.paper: Bolelli et al. localized time-periodic neural-field input
examples.mathematical_object: time-dependent neural field with localized periodic forcing
examples.domain: one- or two-dimensional neural-field domain depending on target figure
examples.parameter_summary: forcing amplitude/frequency and kernel values require final extraction
examples.method: time integration to periodic orbit plus period diagnostics
examples.expected_behavior: periodic response locked to or shaped by localized forcing
examples.rights_status: source figures remain private pending explicit license path
examples.missing_evidence: complete parameter table and period-locking validation rule

examples.label: Bolelli Heaviside flicker stripe diagnostic

examples.symmetry_assumption: localized periodic input seeds stripe-like response
examples.input_type: localized periodic drive with threshold response
examples.parameter_summary: threshold and forcing values require final extraction
examples.method: explicit or semi-implicit time integration with response classification
examples.rights_status: source figures remain private pending explicit license path
examples.missing_evidence: threshold convention, kernel normalization, and source-target metrics

examples.label: Bolelli frequency sweep for localized periodic input
examples.paper: Bolelli et al. localized time-periodic neural-field input
examples.mathematical_object: parameter sweep over time-periodic forced neural-field responses
examples.symmetry_assumption: forcing-localized response measured by generated contours
examples.parameter_summary: frequency and amplitude ranges require final extraction
examples.method: sweep simulation with period-locking and contour-width metrics
